stimulus:
Text 1

Electronic dance music, in which producers assemble tracks using software and audio samples, isn’t really music in any serious sense. It doesn’t demand the kind of skill that performing on a traditional instrument requires. “Composing” on a laptop with a sequencer and a library of loops is far easier than mastering a violin: the software is doing the difficult work.

 

Text 2

The programs used to create electronic dance music involve much more than dragging a few loops together. Beyond knowing the foundations of harmony, rhythm, and arrangement, producers must master complex digital audio workstations, synthesis techniques, and sound design. Many producers begin a track by writing melodies and chord progressions on paper or at a keyboard before shaping them on the computer, where they can apply textures and effects that would otherwise require an entire studio of instruments.
stem: Based on the texts, how would the author of Text 2 most likely respond to the claims of the author of Text 1?

answerOptions.content: By admitting that most electronic producers do not regard traditional musical training as important
By arguing that electronic dance music can still be presented in traditional concert formats
By explaining that producing electronic dance music is actually more difficult than playing many traditional instruments
By maintaining that producing electronic dance music demands musical skill and knowledge even though it relies on less traditional tools

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. Text 1 claims that electronic dance music takes little skill because software does the hard work. Text 2 explicitly counters this, listing the foundations of harmony, rhythm, and arrangement together with mastery of digital audio workstations, synthesis, and sound design as required competencies. The author of Text 2 would therefore insist that producing electronic dance music demands musical skill and knowledge even though it employs less traditional tools.

Choice A is incorrect because Text 2 emphasizes that producers must know the foundations of music. Choice B is incorrect because neither text discusses concert presentation. Choice C is incorrect because Text 2 does not claim that electronic production is more difficult than traditional instruments, only that it requires real skill.
